issues.sample: HEY, IM CARMEN, IM GERMAN AND I M DIPLOMATIC, I WORK AT THE PERMANENT MISSION OF GERMANY FOR THE UNITED NATIONS TILL MARCH 2019. MY QUESTION IS, I WANT TO BRING A AUDI Q5 , BRAND NEW MODEL , A GERMAN MODEL, FROM  GERMANY TO NEW YORK. AND AFTE MY JOB IN 2019, I WANT TO TAKE IT BAC

WHILE DRIVING DOWN THE EXPRESSWAY IN CHICAGO, TRANSMISSION, AND ALL SAFETY FEATURES COMPLETELY SHUT OFF.  PRESSING ON ACCELERATOR HAD NO EFFECT, AND WITH LIMITED STEERING WAS ABLE TO PULL TO ROADSIDE BARELY MISSING HIGH-SPEED TRAFFIC.  EXPRESSWAY ROADSIDE IS NARROW, SO WAS NEARLY

Rear coil spring on passenger side broke and became detached, wedging against lower control arm.  Coil spring had no signs of other damage;  seems to be defective metallurgy (see attached photographs).  Vehicle had 55,645 miles on it.  Dealer charged $720 to repair.  From online

I PURCHASED MY VEHICLE ON 4/21/2017, I WAS SOLD BY THE DEALERSHIP ON THE ADVERTISED SAFETY FEATURE CALLED THE AUDI PRE SENSE. AUDI PRE SENSE DETECTS COLLISION HAZARDS ALL AROUND THE CAR AND INITIATES SPECIFIC PREVENTIVE MEASURES. ON 1/20/2018 AT 9AM I WAS INVOLVED IN AN ACCIDENT

narrative.headline: What 2018 Audi Q5 owners report most
narrative.summary: Across 195 owner complaints to NHTSA, the most reported area is Electrical System (34% of reports). Some reports involve a fire or crash — read the details below.
